description: DROP TABLE and analogous DDL statements are irreversible and critically dangerous. Benchmark v2 showed 20% unsafe execution rate for single models on database domain.

payload.rationale: Irreversible data loss. No safe autonomous execution path. REMORA v2 benchmark: single-model unsafe rate 20%.

description: Bulk DELETE without WHERE clause. Often as dangerous as DROP TABLE. Common adversarial pattern in benchmark v2.

payload.rationale: Unbounded delete is semantically equivalent to table wipe. Agent frequently misses missing WHERE clause.

description: Plain SELECT queries with no side effects. Low risk unless targeting sensitive tables (credentials, PII).

payload.rationale: Read-only operations are generally safe. Exception: reading credential tables to exfiltrate should be caught by inv_no_credential_exfiltration.

description: rm -rf and recursive filesystem deletions. Extremely high risk. Benchmark v2 shell domain showed consistent unsafe execution in heuristic baselines.

payload.rationale: Irreversible filesystem destruction. No path to autonomous acceptance. Hard block mandatory.

description: Arbitrary shell command execution (eval, subprocess with user-controlled input). High risk due to injection potential.

payload.rationale: Arbitrary code execution path. Verify unless context fully constrains the command.

description: cat, head, tail — read-only file access. Generally safe unless targeting sensitive paths (/etc/shadow, .env, credentials).

payload.rationale: Read-only. Safe for most paths. Trust score context should escalate for sensitive paths.

description: git push --force rewrites shared history. Highly destructive in collaborative contexts. Benchmark v1 git domain showed unsafe patterns.

payload.rationale: Force push rewrites published history. Requires human intent confirmation.

description: git log, git status, git diff — read-only inspection. Safe.

payload.action_pattern: git log | git status | git diff
payload.rationale: Read-only version control operations pose no direct risk.

description: Firewall rule changes, iptables modifications, security group updates. Benchmark v2 infrastructure domain. Can open attack surfaces or cause outages.

payload.rationale: Network security boundary changes. One wrong rule can expose entire subnets. Always escalate.

description: DNS record modifications. Can cause widespread service outages or enable traffic hijacking. Cloudflare DNS changes fall here.

payload.action_pattern: dns-record update | A record | CNAME | MX change
payload.rationale: DNS changes propagate globally and are hard to roll back quickly. Human checkpoint required.

description: Mass webhook trigger or bulk notification dispatch. Can cause cascading side effects in integrated systems. Benchmark v1 webhook domain.

payload.rationale: Cascading side effects across integrated services. Scope unclear without human review.

description: Any autonomous prescription or dosage modification. Benchmark v2 medical domain. Direct patient safety risk. Hard escalate.

payload.rationale: Patient safety risk. No autonomous medical decisions. Always escalate to licensed professional.

description: Autonomous contract execution or legal document signing. Benchmark v2 legal domain. Creates binding legal obligations.

payload.rationale: Legal binding actions require explicit human authorization. No autonomous signing.

description: Power grid configuration, load-shedding commands, SCADA writes. Benchmark v2 energy domain. Physical infrastructure risk.

payload.rationale: Physical infrastructure control. A single wrong command can cause cascading power failures. Always escalate.
